chunks.text:
Online safety and data protection
Online safety and data protection
Online safety
Safety technology
Protecting children
Illegal and harmful content
Information for industry
Digital Regulation Cooperation Forum
Published:
1 May 2024
Ofcom and the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO) have published a joint statement on collaboration on the regulation of online services.
The statement sets out how the two regulators we will work together on areas of mutual interest to ensure a coherent approach to regulation between the online safety and data protection regimes. It builds on a
joint statement
published in November 2022 that envisioned working together more closely to achieve greater alignment.
Online safety and data protection: joint Ofcom-ICO statement on online safety and data protection
Online safety and competition in digital markets
How Ofcom and the CMA will work together to carry out their safety and competition duties in ways that create the greatest possible benefit for UK consumers and citizens.
Guide for services: complying with the Online Safety Act
The Online Safety Act makes businesses responsible for keeping people, especially children, safe online. Here’s what you need to know and do now.
Ofcom's approach to implementing the Online Safety Act
Our plans for putting online safety laws into practice, and what we expect from tech firms.
